title: AHA/ACC/ACCP/ASPC/NLA/PCNA Guideline for the Management of Patients With Chronic Coronary Disease
developer_organization: American Heart Association / American College of Cardiology
intended_population: Adults with chronic coronary disease, including stable ischemic heart disease, post-MI, post-revascularization, and ischemic cardiomyopathy.
intended_users: Cardiologists, primary care physicians, nurse practitioners, physician assistants
evidence_quality: Strength of recommendation graded by Class (I-III) and Level of Evidence (A-C)
recommendations_summary: Recommends guideline-directed medical therapy with aspirin, high-intensity statin, ACE inhibitor or ARB (in selected patients), and beta-blocker (post-MI). Endorses revascularization for patients with refractory angina despite optimal medical therapy or specific anatomic indications. Emphasizes lifestyle modification, cardiac rehabilitation, and shared decision-making for invasive testing.

title: U.S. Preventive Services Task Force Recommendation Statement: Screening for Colorectal Cancer
intended_population: Asymptomatic adults aged 45 to 75 years at average risk for colorectal cancer.
evidence_quality: Grade A (ages 50-75) and Grade B (ages 45-49) recommendation
recommendations_summary: Recommends screening for colorectal cancer in adults aged 45 to 75 years. Acceptable strategies include high-sensitivity gFOBT or FIT annually, sDNA-FIT every 1-3 years, CT colonography every 5 years, flexible sigmoidoscopy every 5 years (or every 10 years with annual FIT), and colonoscopy every 10 years.

title: American College of Rheumatology Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis
intended_population: Adults with a diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis, including those with early disease, established disease, or with comorbid conditions.
evidence_quality: GRADE methodology with strong and conditional recommendations
recommendations_summary: Strongly recommends methotrexate as initial DMARD monotherapy for patients with moderate-to-high disease activity. Conditional recommendation to add or switch to a biologic DMARD (TNF inhibitor, IL-6 inhibitor, JAK inhibitor) when methotrexate is inadequate. Treat-to-target approach using validated disease activity measures is strongly recommended.

title: ASCO Guideline Update: Adjuvant Therapy for HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
intended_population: Adults with HER2-positive early-stage breast cancer being considered for adjuvant systemic therapy.
intended_users: Medical oncologists, surgical oncologists, radiation oncologists
evidence_quality: Recommendations rated by evidence strength and quality (high/moderate/low)
recommendations_summary: Recommends adjuvant trastuzumab combined with chemotherapy for patients with HER2-positive tumors >1 cm or with positive lymph nodes. Addition of pertuzumab to trastuzumab and chemotherapy is recommended for high-risk node-positive disease. Neratinib may be offered after trastuzumab-based therapy in hormone-receptor-positive disease at higher risk of recurrence.

title: AAP Clinical Practice Guideline for the Diagnosis, Evaluation, and Treatment of ADHD in Children and Adolescents
intended_population: Children and adolescents aged 4 to 18 years with concerns for ADHD.
evidence_quality: Recommendations graded as Strong, Moderate, or Weak based on quality of evidence
recommendations_summary: Behavior therapy is the first-line treatment for preschool-aged children (4-5 years). For children aged 6-11, FDA-approved medications (stimulants preferred) plus behavioral therapy are recommended. For adolescents (12-18), FDA-approved medications and evidence-based behavior or training therapy are recommended, with patient assent.

title: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
intended_population: Adults and children with type 1 diabetes, type 2 diabetes, gestational diabetes, or prediabetes.
intended_users: Endocrinologists, primary care physicians, diabetes educators, dietitians
evidence_quality: Levels A, B, C and Expert Consensus (E)
recommendations_summary: Recommends individualized glycemic targets (typically A1C <7% for most non-pregnant adults). GLP-1 receptor agonists or SGLT2 inhibitors are preferred when established cardiovascular disease, heart failure, or chronic kidney disease is present. Annual screening for diabetic retinopathy, nephropathy (urinary albumin and eGFR), and neuropathy is recommended.
